output.summary_final: {"title": "Building Trails That Last", "bullets": ["A lot of speed comes from braking earlier and smoother, rather than taking bigger risks.", "A good maintenance routine is essential: check bolts, measure chain wear, clean stanchions, and inspect pads.", "When trails drain well, they remain rideable longer and avoid soil structure damage.", "For climbs, maintaining a consistent cadence reduces drivetrain load and aids in managing traction changes.", "Most mystery creaks can be resolved by cleaning contact surfaces, re-greasing threads, and re-torquing to specifications.", "In wet conditions, decision-making is crucial for protecting traction and trails.", "Riders improve faster by repeating one trail segment and changing only one variable at a time."]}

{"title": "On-Call Practices That Don't Break People", "bullets": ["A good on-call rotation minimizes wake-ups and ensures clear responses when they occur.", "Secrets handling is improved with short-lived, narrowly scoped tokens that are rotated automatically.", "Healthy systems expect failures and manage them with timeouts, retries, and clear ownership.", "Writing down assumptions fosters collaboration and helps resolve disagreements.", "Architecture decisions involve trade-offs between cost, reliability, speed, and correctness.", "Alerting should be action-oriented; if no immediate action can be taken, it shouldn't trigger a page.", "Error budgets are effective when they influence behavior rather than being ignored during planning.", "Start with simple habits and iterate based on observations to improve system resilience."]}

{"title": "Wreck Diving Fundamentals", "bullets": ["The calmest diver is often the safest diver; calmness leads to slow breathing and deliberate decisions.", "For navigation, choose one primary reference and one backup, such as heading plus depth or reef contour plus current direction.", "Good buoyancy control reduces task load and protects the environment, making navigation and photography easier.", "A backup light is essential not just for redundancy but also for mental reassurance during dives.", "Most underwater stress arises from surprise and lack of rehearsal rather than the difficulty of the problem itself.", "Dive plans should be simple, shared, and include clear turn pressures and exit routes.", "Neutral buoyancy drills, like hovering and slow fin pivots, help manage challenging conditions effectively.", "The ability to call a dive early without ego is a crucial skill for divers."]}

{"title": "Mastering Underwater Photography Techniques", "bullets": ["Great images stem from a clear idea and a simple setup executed consistently.", "A small strobe angle change can significantly reduce backscatter by moving the beam away from the lens axis.", "Labeling files and writing captions the same day helps prevent memory gaps in long-term projects.", "In mixed lighting, determine the dominant color temperature and adjust other lights accordingly.", "Documentary work relies on routines, backups, and small habits to avoid catastrophic loss.", "One-light practice is beneficial as it forces learning about light placement and control.", "A grid helps manage light spill, keeping unwanted areas dark and enhancing focus on the subject."]}

{"title": "Index Investing Without the Myths", "bullets": ["Risk is not the same as volatility; it refers to the chance of not meeting goals or suffering permanent loss.", "Position sizing rules are essential to prevent a single great idea from becoming a portfolio-ending mistake.", "Fees compound quietly; lowering costs can effectively increase returns without additional risk.", "Behavior is the hardest part of investing; consistency is crucial during market volatility and negative headlines.", "Diversification should involve different drivers, not just different tickers that move together.", "Drawdowns involve both math and emotion; a 50% loss requires a 100% gain to recover, impacting timelines.", "A simple stress test can help assess if one can handle higher rates, longer vacancies, and unexpected repairs without panic selling.", "Starting simple and documenting observations can help improve investment behavior over time."]}
